slides.title: Distributed Training in Databricks
slides.subtitle: Scaling deep learning from one GPU to the whole cluster
slides.author: Databricks Workshop — Bangalore

slides.title: What we'll cover
items: Why single-node first — and when to scale
DeepSpeed distributor for models that don't fit
Ray Train as the Python-native alternative
Sizing GPU clusters & tracking with MLflow

slides.title: The mental model
slides.subtitle: Start simple. Distribute only when forced.

slides.text: Distributed code is more complex and slower per-step due to communication overhead. Train on a single machine when feasible.

steps.title: Single GPU
steps.description: Model + batch fit on one GPU. Simplest, fastest to iterate.

steps.title: Multi-GPU, one node
steps.description: TorchDistributor local_mode=True for more speed / bigger batch.

steps.title: Multi-node
steps.description: TorchDistributor local_mode=False when one node isn't enough.

steps.title: Model sharding
steps.description: DeepSpeed / ZeRO when the model won't fit in one GPU.

slides.title: Two kinds of parallelism
left: Every worker holds a full model copy
right: The model itself is split across GPUs
Needed when weights exceed one GPU

slides.title: TorchDistributor
slides.subtitle: Databricks' native distributed PyTorch on Spark

slides.title: The same training function scales with one flag
slides.content:
def train_fn(lr, epochs, batch, use_gpu):
    import torch, torch.distributed as dist
    from torch.nn.parallel import DistributedDataParallel as DDP
    dist.init_process_group(backend='nccl')
    # ... DDP model + DistributedSampler ...
    dist.destroy_process_group()

# Single node, multi-GPU:
TorchDistributor(num_processes=4, local_mode=True,  use_gpu=True).run(train_fn, ...)
# Multi-node — only local_mode changes:
TorchDistributor(num_processes=16, local_mode=False, use_gpu=True).run(train_fn, ...)

items.text: Put all imports inside the training function (it gets pickled to workers)
Read LOCAL_RANK / RANK / WORLD_SIZE from environment
Use nccl backend on GPU, gloo on CPU
Wrap model in DistributedDataParallel + use DistributedSampler
Destroy the process group at the end
Requires Spark 3.4+ and Databricks Runtime 13.0 ML+

slides.title: DeepSpeedTorchDistributor
bullets: For low GPU memory & large models
ZeRO shards optimizer state, gradients, params
slides.card_content:
ZeRO stages

Stage 1 — optimizer states
Stage 2 — + gradients
Stage 3 — + parameters

Higher stage = more memory saved, more communication.

slides.title: Ray and Spark are complementary
right: Runs on the same Databricks cluster (2.3.0+)

items.text: Enable the Machine Learning runtime (GPU ML auto-selects)
Leave Photon unchecked — incompatible with GPUs
Inference: use fractions (0.5 / 0.33 / 0.25) to pack tasks per GPU
Prefer on-demand over spot (GPU spot is volatile)
CUDA, cuDNN, NCCL come bundled in the runtime

slides.title: Track everything in MLflow — log from rank 0
slides.content:
import mlflow
with mlflow.start_run(run_name='torchdist-4gpu'):
    mlflow.log_params({'lr': 1e-3, 'world_size': 4, 'strategy': 'ddp'})
    result = TorchDistributor(num_processes=4, local_mode=False, use_gpu=True).run(train_fn, ...)
    mlflow.log_metric('final_loss', result['final_loss'])

items: Model does NOT fit on one GPU
Low GPU memory, very large models
Already using Ray Tune / Data / Serve
Runs on the same Spark cluster

steps.title: PyTorch single-GPU
steps.description: Prove the model trains. Simplest, no distribution.

steps.title: TorchDistributor local_mode=True
steps.description: Data parallelism across GPUs on one node.

steps.title: TorchDistributor local_mode=False
steps.description: Same code, scale across worker nodes.

steps.title: DeepSpeed distributor
steps.description: Switch here only when the model no longer fits one GPU.

slides.title: TorchDistributor vs. DeepSpeed distributor
slides.subtitle: Both run PyTorch on Spark — different problems
left: Problem: training is too slow / batch too small
Each GPU keeps a full model copy
Use when the model fits one GPU
right: Problem: the model won't fit in GPU memory

slides.title: TorchDistributor vs. Ray Train
slides.subtitle: Two ways to do data-parallel deep learning
right: Using Ray Tune / Data / Serve together

slides.title: Key takeaways
bullets: Single-node first — distribution adds complexity and communication cost
Same train_fn goes multi-GPU → multi-node by flipping one flag
DeepSpeed/ZeRO answers 'model won't fit', not 'make it faster'
Ray complements Spark for Python-native scaling
MLflow tracks every run — log from rank 0
